mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and raises refinancing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates restrictive stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months with declining PAYEMS, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,560 thousand, RSAFS at 705,692 million
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower predictive power (Rank IC) than either component alone over a 3-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth acceleration above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding productive capacity and improving supply-side dynamics that support corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO growth > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations, allowing the Federal Reserve to pause or cut rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates compress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% AND FEDFUNDS remains elevated (> 4.5%)
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot from tightening to easing
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows no statistically significant improvement over univariate FEDFUNDS signal (p > 0.10 for interaction term).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerating below 3.0% year-over-year signals successful disinflation that validates the Fed's tightening cycle and reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as terminal rate expectations stabilize.
regime_hint: late-stage monetary tightening with successful disinflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY < 3.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, conditional on inflation remaining controlled (CPIAUCSL YoY < 3.5%).
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 3.5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower predictive power than GDPC1 alone over a 3-year backtest window (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) remaining below 4.0% combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS month-over-month > 0.2%) signals tight labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate hiring, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: full employment regime with sustained labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression predicting positive 5-day returns.
